findings.title: MessageSet parsing allocates attacker-chosen payload buffer before validating availability
findings.description: When a MessageSet item carries field 3 (message bytes) before field 2 (type_id), ParseMessageSetItemImpl reads the payload length with ReadVarint32() and immediately resizes a std::string to `length + VarintSize32(length)`. This path does not use ReadVarintSizeAsInt(), does not compare the length against BytesUntilLimit()/BytesUntilTotalBytesLimit(), and performs the allocation before ReadRaw() verifies the bytes actually exist. A malicious wire payload can therefore request a multi-gigabyte temporary buffer even when the remaining input is much smaller, causing memory exhaustion during parse.
findings.entry_point: Any code path that parses MessageSet wire format through WireFormat::ParseAndMergeMessageSetItem() or ExtensionSet MessageSet parsing on untrusted input.
findings.trigger: A MessageSet item where the embedded message field appears before type_id and advertises a very large length prefix, e.g. near 0xffffffff.

findings.title: Python UnknownFieldSet construction recursively descends groups without a depth limit
findings.description: The upb-backed Python UnknownFieldSet builder creates a new PyUpb_UnknownFieldSet for every start-group field and immediately recurses into PyUpb_UnknownFieldSet_Build() with no recursion counter or limit. Normal protobuf decoding in upb enforces max depth, but this post-parse unknown-field introspection path does not. An attacker can supply a message containing deeply nested unknown groups and then trigger stack exhaustion or interpreter crash when application code calls `google.protobuf.unknown_fields.UnknownFieldSet(message)` or any helper that prints unknown fields.
findings.entry_point: Public Python API `google.protobuf.unknown_fields.UnknownFieldSet(message)`, including text-format printing paths that request unknown fields.
findings.trigger: A parsed message whose unknown field blob contains very deeply nested group fields.

findings.title: Objective-C GPBUnknownFields parsing walks nested groups recursively without using the coded-stream depth guard
findings.description: GPBUnknownFields parses a message's serialized unknown-field data by recursively calling MergeFromInputStream() for every start-group. Unlike normal message/group parsing in GPBCodedInputStream, this helper never calls CheckRecursionLimit() or updates the coded-input recursion accounting before descending. The result is an unbounded recursion path over attacker-controlled unknown groups that can exhaust the Objective-C process stack when code materializes unknown fields from an untrusted message.
findings.entry_point: Public Objective-C API `-[GPBUnknownFields initFromMessage:]` on a message that already contains attacker-controlled unknown fields.
findings.trigger: Unknown field data containing many nested group start/end tags, followed by application code that constructs GPBUnknownFields from the message.
